findings.title: Fire-and-forget sendEmail not safe in Deno edge runtime
findings.why_it_matters: Deno Deploy terminates function execution after the response is flushed. Non-awaited promises (fire-and-forget) are abandoned. The deletion-scheduled email will be silently dropped in production.
findings.suggested_fix: Await sendEmail before the return statement. Email failure should be swallowed (log + continue), not propagated. This satisfies AC-4 while ensuring the email actually sends.
evidence: sendEmail(...).then(...).catch(...) — no await, response returned before promise resolves
Deno Deploy: floating promises without EdgeRuntime.waitUntil() may not complete

findings.title: sendEmail has no timeout — hangs indefinitely on slow provider
findings.why_it_matters: Supabase Edge Functions have a default wall-clock timeout (typically 60s). A slow or unresponsive email provider could hold up the entire delete-account or retention-tick function until it times out, potentially causing the deletion response to fail with a 5xx. If emails were awaited (per P1 fix), this becomes more important.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the fetch in an AbortController with a 10-second timeout: `const controller = new AbortController(); const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 10000); const response = await fetch(providerUrl, { ..., signal: controller.signal }); clearTimeout(timeout);`
evidence: Line 42: `const response = await fetch(providerUrl, { ... })` — no signal/timeout
sendEmail.ts is used in both delete-account (user-facing) and retention-tick (background)

residual_risks: If email provider is consistently unavailable, pending_deletions rows will never be cleaned up (they are only deleted after a successful email attempt — actually after the send attempt, not after confirmed delivery). Rows will accumulate until S11 purge runs.
testing_gaps: No test for sendEmail timeout/abort behavior
No test for the behavior when sendEmail is awaited and throws after the response is already in flight (only relevant if changed to await)
